name: FlicR1
description: Red fluorescent GEVI using the same voltage-sensing domain as ArcLight.

spectrum.name: FlicR1
spectrum.note: FlicR1 is not in FPbase; the displayed curve is the parent fluorophore mApple (the FlicR reporter cpmApple is a circular permutation of mApple). The stored excitation/emission arrays are the FPbase mApple spectrum verbatim (ex 568 nm / em 592 nm). FlicR1's own reported peaks (ex 570 nm / em 597 nm; Abdelfattah et al. 2016, measured on a Safire2 plate reader / DU-800 spectrophotometer) are slightly red-shifted from the mApple proxy curve.

kinetics.note: Biexponential response to a -70→+30 mV step in HEK293 cells (Table 1). Amplitude-weighted means: activation τon = 0.90×3.0 + 0.10×41 = 6.8 ms (τfast 3.0 ms 90%, τslow 41 ms 10%); deactivation τoff = 0.70×2.8 + 0.30×18 = 7.36 ms (τfast 2.8 ms 70%, τslow 18 ms 30%). Previously stored the fast components only (3.0 / 2.8 ms), which understated the response.

Biexponential response to a -70→+30 mV step in HEK293 cells (Table 1). Amplitude-weighted means: activation τon = 0.90×0.74 + 0.10×27 = 3.37 ms (τfast 0.74 ms 90%, τslow 27 ms 10%); deactivation τoff = 0.79×0.93 + 0.21×14 = 3.67 ms (τfast 0.93 ms 79%, τslow 14 ms 21%). Previously stored the fast components only (0.74 / 0.93 ms).

Cross-paper value from the VARNAM/FlicR2 paper (Kannan et al. 2018). Biexponential step-function fit, amplitudes NOT reported, so the 50:50 simple mean is stored (true mean is faster if the fast component dominates): activation = (3.2 + 31.5)/2 = 17.4 ms (τfast 3.2, τslow 31.5); deactivation = (2.6 + 25.8)/2 = 14.2 ms (τfast 2.6, τslow 25.8). HEK293, 561 nm laser 50 mW/mm², 3-5 kHz, n = 3 cells; temperature not stated (room temp assumed for HEK). This is the coarse 50:50 value — the origin-paper amplitude-weighted entries above (34°C 3.4/3.7) remain the displayed kinetics (display prefers ≥33°C).

dynamicRangeData.note: Cross-paper value: −70→+30 mV (100-mV) chord read from the FlicR1 ΔF/V curve in the VARNAM/FlicR2 paper (Kannan et al. 2018, Suppl. Fig. 2e), HEK cells, 1P. The full −70→+50 mV (120-mV) value on that curve is ≈6.4%; the 100-mV chord stored here is ≈5.5%, consistent with the origin-paper 6.6% (Fig. 3D).

brightnessData.note: Resting fluorescence of FlicR1 in HEK cells normalized to the average resting intensity of Ace-mNeon (Kannan et al. 2018, Fig. 1j: FlicR1 ≈ 0.18, FlicR2 ≈ 0.19, Ace-mRuby3 ≈ 0.69, VARNAM ≈ 0.71; Ace-mNeon = 1.0 by definition of the normalization). Read off the bar chart (bar top just below the 0.2 gridline); corrected from a prior 0.15.

photostabilityData.note: Photobleaching time constant τ = 150 ± 10 s (single-exponential fit, Fig. 3J) under continuous 561 nm illumination at 10 W/cm² = 100 mW/mm²; fraction remaining after 1 min = exp(-60/150) = 67%. Corrected illumination from a prior '10 mW/mm²' (a 10× unit error: 1 W/cm² = 10 mW/mm², so 10 W/cm² = 100 mW/mm²). 100 mW/mm² / 1 min matches the reference normalization standard, so no rescaling is applied.

photobleach.note: RE-DIGITIZED 2026-07-03 (color-separated red curve, tick-calibrated: t0 col / 1.0-0.0 rows) from the 1P photobleaching panel Fig. 3I, which normalizes fluorescence vs time for FlicR1 (red), ArcLight Q239 (black), and ASAP1 (green) in HEK293 cells under continuous illumination at 10 W/cm² = 100 mW/mm² (FlicR1 at 561 nm; ASAP1/ArcLight at 488 nm), normalized to 1.0 at t=0; captured every 500 ms (2 Hz). FlicR1 is the MIDDLE curve (between the slower black ArcLight and the fastest green ASAP1) and ends at F ≈ 0.32 at 270 s. The previously stored curve over-bleached the mid-to-late phase (e.g. stored F(90 s)=0.517, F(150 s)=0.36, F(270 s)=0.201 vs the figure's 0.59 / 0.46 / 0.32) and understated t75 (was 40 s); the red-only pixel mask (±0.002 spread per column) confirms the corrected values. Model-free t75 ≈ 48 s (interp; stretched-exponential fit, F=exp(−(t/211.3)^0.834), t75=47 s). The paper's own single-exp fit (τ=150 s, Fig. 3J, stored in reportedTau) predicts t75≈43 s but is a poor fit to the multi-phasic real curve (it ignored the transient photoactivation in the first ~100 s per Methods). Already at the 100 mW/mm² reference, so the dose-scaled value equals the native t75. Cross-plotted with ArcLight Q239 and ASAP1 from the same Fig. 3I (shared source + sourceFigure → cross-overlay); those two companion curves in arclight.json / asap1.json were NOT re-digitized in this pass and should be re-checked against Fig. 3I.

TWO-PHOTON photobleach (standalone — NOT intensity-normalized or comparable to the 1P entry above; no intensityMWmm2). Digitized from Fig. 4C (y = counts/10 ms 0–500, x = time 0–14 s): point-scan fluorescence vs time for FlicR1 excited at 1120 nm (red, F₀ ≈ 343 counts) and ASAP1 at 950 nm (blue, F₀ ≈ 445 counts), ~1 mW at both wavelengths, tuned to ~400 counts per bin initially, sampled at 100 Hz, HEK293 cells; each curve normalized to its own first-frame value. The stored points were re-verified against the figure (agreement within digitization noise: figure gives F(1 s)≈0.78, F(8 s)≈0.53, F(14 s)≈0.46). The three previously stored points at t=16/18/20 s lie BEYOND the figure's 14 s x-axis (fabricated extension) and were removed. A rapid initial drop makes a model-free t75 land in the transient (~1 s), so the sustained-phase t50 ≈ 8.5–8.6 s is stored instead. FlicR1 is ~an order of magnitude more 2P-photostable than ASAP1 (t50 ≈ 8.6 s vs ≈0.7–0.84 s), matching the paper's statement. Cross-plotted with ASAP1 from the same Fig. 4C.

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: FWHM of the mean single-AP optical fluorescence waveform, derived by digitizing the FlicR1 (red, positive-going) trace in Fig. 5E (ΔF/F vs time over the paired electrical AP; 1 kHz frame rate, 10 W/cm² 561 nm, cultured rat hippocampal neurons under whole-cell current clamp). No numeric FWHM is stated in the paper; measured here as the width at half of the baseline-to-peak amplitude (peak ≈ 20 ms, half-max span ≈ 17.9–24.7 ms → FWHM ≈ 6.8 ms), overlay-verified. FlicR1's optical AP is sharp and closely tracks the electrical spike (paper: 'not broadened to the extent that it was with ArcLight Q239'; cf. the cross-GEVI ArcLight entry ≈ 70 ms). Recording temperature not stated for this neuron experiment.
